issues.feedback: The design jumps straight to a custom ref walker, but `schemars` already supports inlining via `SchemaSettings::inline_subschemas`. The spec should at least compare against that option or explain why backend-local post-processing is preferable; otherwise it is not clear this is the minimum mechanism.

Acceptance criterion 5 is not actually verified. The proposed tests only call `inline_schema_refs()` directly, while the regression risk is the `build_command()` wiring. Reuse existing command-construction coverage in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs`, or add same-module `build_command()` tests that assert the Claude `--json-schema` payload and Codex schema file contain no `$ref`/`definitions` for a schema that really produces refs.

The helper is defined as shared backend-facing schema normalization next to `enforce_strict_mode_schema()`, but the spec excludes `src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs`, which already reuses the strict-mode helper and also sends strict JSON Schema. Either include OpenRouter or explicitly document why `$ref` is safe there and why only Claude/Codex should diverge.

The algorithm does not define behavior for malformed or partial inputs: missing ref targets, a non-object top-level `definitions`, or schemas that have `definitions` but no matching `$ref`. It also implicitly assumes pure `{"$ref":"#/definitions/X"}` nodes. The spec should state whether these cases are no-ops or errors so the implementation can avoid panics and the acceptance criteria stay unambiguous.
